name: pdf2image (PDF to PIL/Pillow Image Converter)

auth.notes: Local file processing via poppler; no auth

performance.notes: Poppler subprocess; ~200ms per page at 150dpi. Higher DPI = slower.

agent_readiness.notes: Convert PDF pages to PIL Image objects. from pdf2image import convert_from_path. images = convert_from_path('doc.pdf'). → list of PIL.Image.Image. images[0].save('page1.jpg'). DPI: convert_from_path('doc.pdf', dpi=300). First page only: first_page=1, last_page=1. convert_from_bytes(pdf_bytes). Thread-safe: use_cropbox, strict parameter. fmt='JPEG' or 'PNG'. Output path: output_folder='./output'. Requires poppler system package: brew install poppler (macOS), apt install poppler-utils (Linux). Used in OCR pipelines: pdf2image + pytesseract. Also: use_pdftocairo=True for better quality.

gotchas: Requires poppler system package — not a pure Python solution
Memory usage scales with DPI * page count — large PDFs at high DPI can OOM
Not streaming — entire page list returned at once (use first_page/last_page for pagination)
Poppler not available on Windows by default — needs manual install or conda
PDFs with passwords need pypdf/pikepdf to decrypt first
best_when: Extracting specific pages from PDFs as images
avoid_when: Text extraction from PDFs (use pdfplumber or pypdf)
When poppler installation is not feasible (serverless/restricted envs)
High-volume production at high DPI (memory and speed constraints)
summary: pdf2image converts PDF pages to PIL Images via poppler. from pdf2image import convert_from_path; images = convert_from_path('doc.pdf', dpi=150); images[0].save('page.jpg'). Page range: first_page=1, last_page=5. MIT. Requires poppler system package (brew/apt). Best for OCR pipelines and PDF thumbnails. Use pdfplumber/pypdf for text extraction.
